Authored by GPT-5.5 (Codex Desktop). Session ea6898ba-531f-4162-996b-99e7b235c120.

Resolves #10498

## Summary
- Adds `wakeSuppressed` to `add_message` / `MailboxService.addMessage()` so mailbox-only continuity pings persist unread without producing `SENT_TO_ME` wake events.
- Suppresses wake emission in both `WakeSubscriptionService` and the bridge daemon GraphLog poller.
- Mirrors routing and filter metadata onto MESSAGE nodes (`from`, `to`, `taggedConcepts`, etc.) so existing wake filters can evaluate MailboxService-created messages.
- Updates `session-sunset` and startup guidance so sunset self-DMs are sent with `wakeSuppressed: true` and picked up by next-session `list_messages`.

## Test Evidence
- `git diff --check`
- `npm run test-unit -- test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/memory-core/services/MailboxService.spec.mjs test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/memory-core/services/WakeSubscriptionService.spec.mjs test/playwright/unit/ai/scripts/bridge-daemon.spec.mjs --workers=1`

Note: the targeted test command required elevated filesystem permissions locally because `.neo-ai-data/sqlite` is a symlink to the shared data directory outside the Codex sandbox.

## Post-Merge Validation
- [ ] A future session-sunset call can pass `wakeSuppressed: true`; no active same-session wake is emitted, and the next boot `list_messages` sees the unread handover.

## Commit
- `c6f5221de` — `fix(memory-core): suppress sunset self-DM wakes (#10498)`

> ### 🔬 Depth Floor
> 
> **Challenge OR documented search (per guide §7.1):**
> **Challenge:** The `wakeSuppressed` flag is currently permitted on any message (including `AGENT:*` broadcast or DMs to peers) rather than strictly limited to self-DMs. While this provides flexibility (e.g., silent polling updates), it introduces the risk of agents sending "stealth" DMs to peers that bypass the wake substrate entirely. This is acceptable for now but something to watch.
>
